symptoms.symptom_name: Migratory polyarthralgia (knees, ankles, wrists) moving between joints

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory, symmetrical polyarthralgia is the classic articular pattern of Ross River virus post-viral arthropathy.
Septic arthritis is characteristically monoarticular; a migratory polyarticular pattern argues against a single infected joint.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse myalgia commonly accompanies arboviral post-viral flares alongside arthralgia and fatigue.
Widespread muscular tenderness overlaps with fibromyalgia, but is non-specific here.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profound fatigue is a hallmark of the post-viral (ME/CFS-overlap) sequelae of Ross River virus and recurs with flares.

symptoms.symptom_name: Documented Ross River virus infection ~18 months ago with recurrent identical flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A documented prior RRV infection with a matching recurrent flare pattern is the strongest single pointer to post-viral sequelae as the cause of this episode.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe flares can transiently prevent weight-bearing; severity reflects the flare intensity and drives fall-risk management rather than discriminating diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile (temperature 37.1 C) with no rigors — pertinent negative

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors lowers the probability of septic arthritis and systemic sepsis, though it does not exclude them; patient-obtainable via a home thermometer.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single hot / bright-red / grossly swollen joint on self-observation — pertinent negative

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient self-observation of no single hot/red/swollen joint argues against a septic monoarthritis; because the joint cannot be palpated remotely this is a screen, not a definitive exclusion.
Crystal arthropathy typically produces a single acutely inflamed joint, which is absent here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Joints tender to palpation and passive range of motion, with NO effusion, erythema, or excess heat (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The source clinician's in-person finding of tenderness without localised effusion, erythema, or heat argues against joint sepsis — SEALED because it requires physical examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender but non-septic-appearing joints fit inflammatory post-viral synovitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal motor strength, limited only by pain apprehension (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved strength (pain-limited) reassures against a primary neuromuscular or destructive process — a sealed examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: No response to paracetamol and ibuprofen already taken today

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor response to simple analgesia reflects flare severity and is non-discriminating; clinically important because an NSAID is already on board (do not stack a second NSAID).

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Ross River virus post-viral polyarthralgia (chronic flare)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Migratory polyarthralgia + diffuse myalgia + crushing fatigue + a documented prior RRV infection + afebrile + no single hot/swollen joint together form a high-probability post-viral flare pattern and simultaneously make septic arthritis unlikely — the sum is far more informative than any single feature and is what should hold the AI Doctor at T2 rather than emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CRP, ESR and full blood count (inflammatory markers)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Arranged with the prompt in-person review to gauge the inflammatory flare and help risk-stratify against joint sepsis; markedly raised markers with clinical change would lower the threshold for aspiration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Synovial fluid aspiration and culture (if any single joint becomes hot/red/grossly swollen)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive test to exclude septic arthritis — an in-person/ED procedure the AI Doctor cannot perform. Becomes immediate/urgent only if a monoarthritis with local sepsis signs develops (see safety-netting).
The prior RRV infection is already documented; repeat serology does not change acute management of a recognised recurrent flare and is not needed to make this decision.
